summary: The JUnit test DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed with an assertion error at line 60: the test expected owner version not to be incremented (false) but the actual result was true, indicating changed version-handling behavior in Hibernate collection operations.
root_cause: Test assertion at DbVersionTest.java:60 failed with expected: <false> but was: <true> for owner version increment check, indicating a mismatch between expected and actual entity versioning behavior in collection operations.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: Direct assertion failure showing the expected boolean value (false) did not match the actual value (true)
Pinpoints the exact file and line where the assertion failed during test execution
Only one test failed among thousands, confirming this is an isolated test assertion failure, not a build or environment-wide issue
Test was executed and failed at runtime due to assertion mismatch, not compilation or setup failure

suggested_fix: Review recent code changes affecting entity versioning behavior in collection operations to identify if a regression was introduced that now increments owner version when it should not.
